frames: The agent starts in an upright posture, then its lower segment begins to tilt to the right.
The lower segment bends further to the right, while the upper segment maintains a mostly vertical orientation.
The agent's lower segment is significantly angled to the right, and the upper segment starts to lean slightly.
The lower segment is now nearly horizontal, and the entire agent is leaning far to its right.
The agent reaches its maximum lean, with the lower segment almost parallel to the ground and the upper segment sharply angled.
The agent smoothly begins to recover, lifting its lower segment from the extreme right position.
The agent continues to straighten, with both segments moving back towards a vertical alignment.
The agent returns to an almost upright posture, maintaining a slight forward lean.
